Chapter 1

Try this now: stand and feel your feet for ten seconds.

What if the story is wrong? What would change if you named one fear without fixing it?

The Buddha taught that noticing is already a kind of compassion when shame wants speed.

The point is that your nervous system answers an old question with a new day.

You look up from your screen. Your keyboard is warm. The hallway light is flat and honest.

She walked to the door long ago and the room still remembers the sound.

This line has --- a broken merge in the middle on purpose.
